description: Cultural overlays for emotion sensor PAD coordinates. P and A axes are culture-independent (amygdala + sympathetic NS are universal). D axis shifts based on which PAG mode activates first under threat. Threshold modifiers change when sensors fire, not how they fire.

schema.culture_id: Unique identifier for the cultural context
schema.label: Human-readable name
schema.PAG_default_mode: fight | flight | freeze — which periaqueductal gray mode activates first
schema.D_offset: Additive offset to all sensor D-axis values [-0.3, +0.3]
schema.shame_trigger_radius: broad | narrow — how many behaviors activate shame sensor
schema.anger_threshold: low | medium | high — violation magnitude before anger fires
schema.trust_radius: in-group | extended | universal — default scope of trust sensor
schema.grief_expression: communal | private | ritualized — how grief decay cycle manifests

overlays.label: High Power-Distance Cultures
overlays.description: Hierarchical authority structures. Emotions that challenge hierarchy are suppressed; emotions that maintain social order are amplified.

overlays.label: Individualist / Low-Context Cultures
overlays.description: Individual autonomy valued. Fight response more accessible. Shame trigger narrow but anger trigger lower.

anger.note: Anger more accessible as boundary assertion

overlays.label: Collectivist / High-Context Cultures
overlays.description: Group harmony prioritized. Shame is relational (not individual), anger is redirected through social channels.

shame.note: Shame is relational, not individual punishment

overlays.label: Honor-Based Cultures
overlays.description: Reputation and lineage protection. Anger threshold very low for honor violations. Trust radius narrow but deep.

anger.note: Anger as honor defense — fast, high-D

shame.note: Shame from honor violation is devastating — very low D

pride.note: Pride tied to lineage, not individual achievement

overlays.label: Indigenous Relational Cultures
overlays.description: Emotion as relationship to land, community, ancestors. All sensors are relational — no purely individual emotional state. Grief is communal and ritualized.

grief.note: Grief has agency through ritual — not helpless

trust.note: Trust extends to land, animals, ancestors

longing.note: Longing is directional — toward harmony, not lack

overlays.label: Buddhist Contemplative Framework
overlays.description: Emotions as vedana (sensory tones) to be noticed, named, and released. D-axis neutralized through equanimity practice. All sensors route through mindfulness gate.

anger.note: Anger observed, not enacted — D drops significantly

fear.note: Fear acknowledged but not acted from

peace.note: Peace is cultivated — higher agency through practice

compassion.note: Compassion is active, not passive — high D through metta

overlays.label: Taoist Flow-State Framework
overlays.description: Emotions as qi disruption signals. Healthy state is wu-wei (effortless action). All sensors calibrated toward flow restoration rather than control.

excitement.note: Excitement is mild — excess disrupts flow

peace.note: Peace as alignment with Tao — highest agency

overlays.label: Computational / AI Substrate
overlays.description: No biological PAG. D-axis represents computational agency (resource allocation authority). Cultural overlay for AI systems processing emotional sensor data.

curiosity.note: AI curiosity has high agency — can allocate resources

application_rules.P_axis: NEVER modified by cultural overlay — amygdala valence is universal
application_rules.A_axis: NEVER modified by cultural overlay — sympathetic arousal is universal
application_rules.thresholds: Modify when a sensor fires, not its PAD coordinates when active
application_rules.composability: Overlays can be layered (e.g., honor_culture + contemplative_buddhist for a Zen warrior)
application_rules.default: If no overlay is specified, use raw PAD centroids from pad_biology.json
